summary: Under constant acceleration the velocity changes uniformly with time: v = v₀ + a·t. Use it whenever an object speeds up or slows down at a steady rate.
method: List the four quantities — final velocity v, initial velocity v₀, acceleration a, time t — and mark which one is unknown.
Make sure the acceleration is constant; this equation does not apply if a changes.
Rearrange v = v₀ + a·t for the unknown.
Keep signs consistent: choose a positive direction, and make a negative if the object is slowing down.
Substitute in SI units (m/s, m/s², s) and compute.

example.title: A car speeding up
steps: The unknown is v, so use v = v₀ + a·t.
